commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat futures $6.05-$6.10/bu (April 25). Hit highest since March 31. USDA: only 30% winter wheat rated good/excellent (down from 45% YoY). Kansas drought. Supply concerns.
commodities.source: Grain Central — Daily Market Wire April 24

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn $4.625/bu July futures (April 25). Acreage down to 95.3M from 99M due to fertilizer costs. Elevated diesel and transportation costs.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans $11.60/bu (April 25). Supported by biofuel demand as crude disruptions boost biodiesel. Soybean oil up 15.6% in past month.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: ICE Sugar #11 at $0.224/lb (April 25). Up 6.7% from pre-war. USDA forecasts sugar & sweets +6.7% in 2026.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica $2.94/lb (Apr 10). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41 but still elevated. Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.note: USDA projects food-at-home prices to increase 3.1% in 2026 — nearly double original projection. Wheat surging (+8% from pre-war, KC wheat +29¢ on Kansas drought). Diesel-dependent supply chains passing fuel costs to consumers. 1/3 of globally traded fertilizer flows through Hormuz. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points.

fertilizer.detail: Approximately one-third of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea surged to $650+/MT (+~50% in 3 weeks, some spot markets $800+). Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA projects 3.1% food price rise in 2026. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Critical Northern Hemisphere spring planting season underway.

context: Day 57 PM — Brent $104.50 (+30.9% from $79.84). WTI $94.65 (+25.4%). Wheat $6.07 (+8.0%). Corn $4.63 (+7.4%). Soybeans $11.60 (+14.6%). USDA: food-at-home prices to rise 3.1% in 2026 (nearly double original projection). Fertilizer crisis: urea $650+/MT. Gas $4.03 AAA (-6¢ WoW).
